modules.name: Cascade
modules.description: Granovetter threshold model of collective behavior. N agents with heterogeneous activation thresholds; an external Pressure signal pushes them past their tipping points one cascade at a time. Outputs active fraction, delta, ignition gate, and per-agent gates.

modules.name: Discourse
modules.description: Deffuant-Weisbuch bounded-confidence convergence model. N agents hold continuous values on [0,1]; at each tick random pairs interact only if their values are within a confidence threshold ε, and pull toward each other by μ. Over time the population fragments into 1..N stable value clusters. Outputs polyphonic values, mean, variance, cluster count.

modules.name: Pareto
modules.description: Stochastic pairwise exchange model (Chakraborti / Boghosian). N agents hold a positive value; random pairs exchange a fraction of the smaller value. Even with fair coin flips, the value condenses on a single agent — adding BIAS (advantage to the higher side) accelerates concentration; adding POOL (mean-field redistribution) creates non-trivial steady-state distributions. Outputs polyphonic value shares, Gini coefficient, top share, concentration gate.

modules.name: Dilemma
modules.description: Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma round-robin tournament. N agents (assigned to one of four strategies: ALL_C / ALL_D / TFT / GRIM) play pairwise PD each round. MIX biases strategy distribution; PAYOFF tilts the temptation term; NOISE adds action errors that break TFT's nice cooperation. Outputs per-agent cooperation gates, mean cooperation, average score, sucker gate.

modules.name: Diffusion
modules.description: Bass innovation-adoption dynamics. N agents are either adopters or not; each tick each non-adopter has probability p + q·f of adopting, where p is the innovation rate (spontaneous), q is the imitation rate (peer-driven), and f is the current adoption fraction. Produces the canonical S-curve: slow early uptake driven by innovators, accelerating middle phase driven by imitators, plateau as the pool of non-adopters thins. Outputs per-agent adoption gates, fraction adopted, adoption rate, saturation gate. Adoption is irreversible (consistent with the standard Bass model).

modules.name: Network
modules.description: Relational structure generator. Builds an N-agent undirected graph in one of three canonical models: Watts-Strogatz small-world (ring lattice with β rewiring), Erdős–Rényi random graph (each edge present with probability β), or Barabási–Albert preferential attachment (scale-free growth, K seed connections per new node). Outputs polyphonic per-agent degree, mean degree, global clustering coefficient, and a connected-graph gate. The visualization shows the network laid out on a circle with edges between nodes — useful both standalone and as a structural pre-requisite for diffusion-on-networks pedagogy.
